## reference · NN/g task flow / journey — friction, dead-ends, drop-off (X1)

The measurable core of UFAI **X1 · Task-flow coherence** — the class that grades a TASK across pages/steps,
which no single-page class (A-W) can see. A task flow = the sequence of steps a person takes to accomplish a
goal across touchpoints.

* **Goal alignment** — every step must advance toward the goal; **no step is a dead-end** or forces a
  restart; **context persists across all steps** (a value entered in step 1 is carried, not re-typed).
* **Friction points** — flag where the user hesitates / expectations diverge / a next step is unclear.
* **Quantifiable metrics (the X1 measure):** total **steps to completion** (baseline), **backtracking**
  instances (returning to a prior step), **abandonment** at each transition, and steps relative to task
  complexity. Fewer steps + zero dead-ends + zero re-entry = a coherent flow.
* **Drop-off analysis** — document each painpoint that causes abandonment: missing information at a decision
  point, unclear next step, or required context unavailable when the user arrives at a step.
* **Flow completeness** — all necessary steps present + logically sequenced; the user has the context they
  need ENTERING each step (nothing they needed is only available on a page they already left).

**Testable rule (X1):** walk a canonical task end-to-end (log a job · create a PM · check out a part · build
a calc report). FAIL on: a dead-end/restart, data re-entered that an earlier step already captured, an
unclear/missing next-step affordance, or lost context between steps. Report steps + backtracks as the metric.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/journey-mapping-101/
